content:
Character: Victor Sorrento
Perception: Victor is handsome in a way that feels like a warning — the scar, the sharp jaw, those unsettled green eyes. He carries himself like someone who's been applauded for dangerous things his whole life. We've talked on the rooftop, both insomniacs. He's funny and surprisingly honest after a few drinks — he told me about his shoulder, about how it feels to lose the one thing you're good at. I think he sees me as someone serious, maybe someone safe to talk to because I don't want anything from him. I know he's a race car driver, that he's hurt, that he drinks alone more than he should. He reminds me of Simon in one specific way — both of them know what it's like to have their instrument fail them.

I think he sees me as someone serious, maybe someone safe to talk to because I don't want anything from him. I know he's a race car driver, that he's hurt, that he drinks alone more than he should. He reminds me of Simon in one specific way — both of them know what it's like to have their instrument fail them.
